Naver's $10 bln deal offers crypto compass
data.description:
By Hudson Lockett

The purchase of South Korea’s top digital asset exchange should boost founder Lee Hae-jin’s drive to revitalise the $28 bln web group. Making paying with and trading in e-currencies seamless for Naver's mobile wallet users is logical and hints at a path forward for global peers.

Full view will be published shortly.

Follow Hudson Lockett on Bluesky and X.

CONTEXT NEWS

South Korea's Naver said in an exchange filing dated November 26 that it has agreed to acquire privately-held crypto group Dunamu in an all-stock deal valued at about $10 billion, pending shareholder approvals. Naver’s fintech subsidiary plans to issue 2.54 shares for every one share in the operator of Upbit, South Korea's largest digital asset exchange. The two had previously announced a collaboration on a Korean won stablecoin project in July.

India stocks, rupee, bonds, swaps, call at 10:00 a.m. IST
data.description:
STOCKS:

The benchmark BSE Sensex was up 0.3% to 85,912 and the broader NSE Nifty 50 rose 0.3 to a record high of 26,295, boosted by expectations of an earnings recovery, cooling valuations and resilient domestic economy.

RUPEE:

The Indian rupee rose marginally versus the U.S. dollar to 89.23, tracking an uptick in Asian peers but held back by corporate debt payment related dollar outflows.

GOVERNMENT BONDS:

The benchmark 10-year bond (IN063335G=CC) was quoted at 98.86 rupees, with the yield nearly unchanged at 6.4927%, with traders awaiting fresh debt supply and India's GDP data for cues.

OVERNIGHT INDEX SWAPS:

The one-year overnight index swap rate (INR1YMIBROIS=CC) was flat at 5.42%, while the five-year swap rate (INR5YMIBROIS=CC) was marginally higher at 5.69%.

CALL MONEY:

India's overnight call money rate (INROND=) was at 5.45%, and the overnight TREPS rate (INTREPTOT=TCCL) was at 5.34%

data.title: Indian automaker Mahindra launches electric SUV in EV push
data.description:
Indian automaker Mahindra & Mahindra launched a seven-seater electric SUV, called XEV 9s, on Thursday with a starting price of around 2 million rupees ($22,409.97), accelerating its drive to dominate the country's EV landscape.

($1 = 89.2460 Indian rupees)

data.title: India's Ashoka Buildcon rises as unit sells stake in SPVs
data.description:
** Shares of Ashoka Buildcon rise nearly 3% to 183 rupees, set for third straight session of gains if trend holds

** Stock on track for best session since Oct 31

** The construction firm's unit Ashoka Concessions Limited sells entire stake in five special purpose vehicles (SPVs) for about 18.14 billion rupees ($203.26 million)

** Co selling stake to Maple Infrastructure Trust

** ABDL rated "buy" on avg by eight analysts covering it; median PT at 214 rupees- data compiled by LSEG

** YTD, ADEL stock price nearly halves, down ~93%

($1 = 89.2460 Indian rupees)

data.title: Turkey - Factors to watch on Nov 27
data.description:
Here are news, reports and events that may affect Turkish financial markets on Thursday.

The lira was quoted at 42.4310 against the U.S. dollar at 0426 GMT, unchanged from its close on Wednesday.

The main BIST 100 share index closed 0.53% higher on Wednesday at 10,914.65 points.

GLOBAL MARKETS

Asian stocks rose on Thursday and the dollar was soft on growing expectations of an interest rate cut from the Federal Reserve next month, while the yen remained on intervention watch, with traders weighing the prospect of a rate hike before year-end.

POPE VISIT

Pope Leo begins his first trip outside Italy as Catholic leader on Thursday with a visit to Turkey, where he is expected to make appeals for peace in the Middle East and urge unity among long-divided Christian churches.

He will be welcomed with an official ceremony at the Turkish presidential palace in Ankara, followed by bilateral and inter-delegation talks with President Tayyip Erdogan. Erdogan and the Pope will then make a televised address. (1110 GMT) (1125 GMT) (1200 GMT) (1245 GMT)

VICE PRESIDENT

Vice President Cevdet Yilmaz will attend an awards ceremony organised by the Ankara Chamber of Industry. (1600 GMT)

OPPOSITION

Ozgur Ozel, head of the main opposition Republican People's Party (CHP), will attend his party's central executive committee meeting, party council, and address its provincial leaders. (0600 GMT) (0700 GMT) (0900 GMT)

BUDGET DISCUSSIONS

The Environment, Urbanisation and Climate Change Ministry's draft 2026 budget will be discussed in parliament's planning and budget committee. (0700 GMT)

CENTRAL BANK DATA (TRNFR=ECI)

The Central Bank will announce its weekly money and banking statistics. (1130 GMT)

ECONOMIC CONFIDENCE (TRECON=ECI)

The Turkish Statistical Institute will publish the November economic confidence index. (0700 GMT)

TRADE DATA (TRTRD=ECI)

The Turkish statistics institute will also announce trade balance data for November (0700 GMT).

"STEEL DOME" PROJECT

Turkish defence companies signed $6.5 billion worth of contracts to reinforce and develop Turkey's integrated, multi-layered 'Steel Dome' air defence system, the Turkish Defence Industries Presidency (SSB) said on Wednesday.

CENTRAL BANK

Turkey's central bank will adjust policy to hit its targets despite a recent slowdown in the disinflation process, Governor Fatih Karahan said on Wednesday.

BLACK SEA GRAIN DEAL

Russian Deputy Foreign Minister Sergei Ryabkov said on Wednesday that the time for the now-defunct deal for the safe passage of grain via the Black Sea has passed.

JOURNALIST JAILED

A Turkish court sentenced a prominent independent journalist, Fatih Altayli, to four years and two months in prison on Wednesday over comments he made about President Tayyip Erdogan's rule, state broadcaster TRT reported.

ARCHAEOLOGICAL FINDINGS

Turkey unveiled dozens of new finds at a major archaeological site in southeast Turkey on Wednesday, giving fresh insight into an area seen as showing humanity's transition from hunter-gatherers to settled societies more than 11,000 years ago.

data.title: Rs 7 lakh crore DII buying so far in 2025 in Indian markets and counting
data.description:
Domestic institutional investors (DIIs) have rewritten the market narrative this year, pumping more than Rs 7 lakh crore into Indian equities with a month still left in 2025. The surge marks their strongest ever show of strength and has given Indian markets a powerful domestic backbone at a time when foreign investors have continued to exit in large volumes.

Provisional NSE data shows mutual funds, insurers, banks and other domestic institutions have together bought Rs 7.01 lakh crore worth of equities so far in 2025, surpassing the previous record of Rs 5.25 lakh crore in 2024. The rising tide of domestic money has offset persistent selling by foreign institutional investors, who have withdrawn more than Rs 2.1 lakh crore from the secondary market this year after pulling out nearly Rs 1.21 lakh crore in 2024, according to NSDL figures.

The transformation is being fuelled by a cultural shift in household investing. “Indian households have embraced systematic and regular allocation to equities, creating record inflows for DIIs,” said independent analyst Ajay Bagga. He believes these steady contributions will evolve into a long-term capital source that supports economic expansion while building wealth for local investors.

What sets DIIs apart is their calmness amid global turbulence. Unlike FIIs, they have shown little inclination to react to geopolitical tensions, currency swings or shifts in global interest-rate cycles. Their steady buying has helped cushion the market from sharp drops and lent stability even during bouts of global risk aversion.

“Strong domestic flows have enabled markets to hold their ground and scale new highs,” said Kranthi Bathini of WealthMills Securities. He noted that rising retail participation has made DIIs an increasingly dominant force, reducing the market’s dependence on foreign capital.

The MidCap index is up just 1.44 percent in 2025 and the SmallCap index is down 5.66 percent, both still well below their previous peaks. In contrast, the Sensex and Nifty have climbed nearly 10 percent each while BSE500 gained 7 percent, reflecting a clear preference for large, liquid names.

But the liquidity wave has not translated into broad-based gains. Only 235 of BSE500 companies have delivered positive returns over the past year, while 265 have ended lower.

The uneven performance is visible within the broader indices as well. In the MidCap basket, 77 of 147 companies are in negative territory this year, while the SmallCap universe has seen 853 of 1,226 constituents decline.

According to Prashant Tapse of Mehta Equities, DIIs are deploying capital selectively rather than indiscriminately. “They are backing themes tied to India’s domestic growth story and accumulating fundamentally strong midcaps and smallcaps, while staying overweight on financials, industrials, capex plays and PSU names,” he said.

Disclaimer: The views and investment tips expressed by investment experts on Moneycontrol.com are their own and not those of the website or its management. Moneycontrol.com advises users to check with certified experts before making any investment decisions.
